_comment: Assets a plain shell cannot fetch (registration form / EULA / login / JS-driven download). scripts/setup checks presence and lists missing ones in tmp/setup-state.json; the Claude path (docs/onboarding.md) drives the browser MCP to fetch them. Every GitHub-release DFIR binary is curl-able and handled by scripts/install-dfir-tools.sh instead, so it does NOT belong here. To add a binary later, use kind:binary with destination.dir ~/.local/bin and env_var $<TOOL>_BIN.

tools.name: SANS SIFT Workstation OVA

steps: navigate landing_url, then read the href of the anchor whose accessible name is 'Download' near the 'SIFT Workstation' heading (href matches sansorg.egnyte.com/dl/). Never hardcode the token — it rotates when SANS updates the OVA.
navigate that Egnyte share URL (a public file share — no login).
click the 'Download' button (button accessible name 'Download') to start the file download.
browser.extract: The /dl/<token> URL itself returns text/html (the Egnyte share UI), not the file. The actual file is served only after the Download button click, via a dynamic, time-limited signed URL bound to the page session/cookies — so it cannot be captured once and stored; resolve it at download time.

fetch.download_dir: tmp/gated-downloads — point the browser here via CDP Page.setDownloadBehavior (or set FINDEVIL_DOWNLOAD_DIR) BEFORE clicking Download, so the OVA never lands in ~/Downloads.
fetch.browser_download: click Download (download dir set as above), wait until the .ova file size is stable (no .crdownload), then run the place_helper to move it into the project location.
fetch.place_helper: bash scripts/place-gated-download.sh sift-ova — moves a finished download from the controlled dir or ~/Downloads to <repo-root>/sift-<version>.ova (idempotent; rejects HTML/partial files). Run this even if the download already went to ~/Downloads.
fetch.curl_alt: intercept the file-stream request triggered by the Download click (the signed URL) and run curl -fL --cookie <session-cookies> immediately; the URL is time-limited so resume across expiry is not guaranteed. Plain curl on the /dl/ URL returns HTML and will NOT work.
fetch.notes: no SANS credentials required (verified public share).

recon.method: Playwright MCP navigate + accessibility snapshot of the SANS page and the Egnyte share; curl HEAD/1-byte probe of the /dl/ URL.

observed.sans_download_href: https://sansorg.egnyte.com/dl/<token> (public anchor; token rotates per OVA update)

tools.manual_fallback: Open the landing_url, click Download (it leads to a public Egnyte share — no login), save the OVA as sift-<version>.ova at the repo root (or set OVA_PATH=/path/to/your.ova). Then re-run: bash scripts/setup.
